topic.name: Federal Legislative Acts
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative acts aimed at improving education, healthcare, military assistance, and addressing violence against women.

Document 1: Reauthorizes through FY2019 the programs under titles I-IV of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965
Expresses the sense of Congress that states and local educational agencies (LEAs) retain the rights and responsibilities of determining educational curricula
Document 2: Authorizes FY2009-FY2010 appropriations for the foreign military financing program
Amends the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 to authorize FY2009-FY2010 appropriations for international military education and training.
Document 3: Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to study and report on the system of third-party payment collections for items and services furnished through the Indian Health Service
Amends the Indian Health Care Improvement Act to declare a new national Indian health policy
Document 4: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 to add or expand definitions of several terms
Modifies or expands grant conditions under such Act
Document 5: Amends the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 to extend from 18 to 48 months the length of time a designated state unit may provide supported employment services
Authorizes the Commissioner of the Rehabilitation Services Administration of the Department of Education to make up to ten grants to or contracts with eligible entities
Document 6: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 to add or expand definitions of several terms
Requires the Office on Violence Against Women of the Department of Justice to establish a biennial conferral process
Document 7: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to require each institution of higher education to include in its annual security report a statement of current policies for reporting crimes
Requires schools to protect victim confidentiality when reporting criminal threats to the campus community

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments aimed at modifying existing laws related to healthcare, trade, taxation, and compliance regulations.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
define a 'full-time employee' as an employee who is employed on average at least 40 hours per week
Document 2: Amends the Trade Act of 1974
extend duty-free treatment under the Generalized System of Preferences (GSP)
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
modify the article description relating to the duty-free treatment
Document 4: Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995
increase from $50,000 to $200,000 the civil fine for noncompliance
Document 5: amends the Social Security Act
prohibit the Social Security Administration from issuing a Social Security account number
Document 6: Directs the United States Customs and Border Protection
liquidate or reliquidate certain entries of large newspaper printing presses
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States
extend the temporary suspension of duty on oysters

topic.name: Election Reform and Integrity
topic.description: The documents focus on various aspects of reforming and ensuring the integrity of electoral processes in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act
revise and distribute powers of the FEC
Document 2: restores the right of District of Columbia residents to participate in elections
repeals provisions establishing the office of District of Columbia Delegate
Document 3: amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971
establishment of a House Clean Elections Fund
Document 4: prohibits redistricting until after the next apportionment
requires establishment of a state independent redistricting commission
Document 5: requires states to meet specified standards for transparent and accurate tabulation of votes
mandatory hand recount in case of unresolved discrepancy
Document 6: requires registered lobbyists to file quarterly reports
defines covered recipient as federal candidates and political committees
Document 7: requires the Supreme Court to promulgate a code of ethics for the Justices
includes the five canons of the Code of Conduct for United States Judges

topic.name: Tariff Suspension Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to the temporary suspension of duties on various goods in the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States.

Document 1: extends the temporary suspension of duty on acrylic staple fiber
with a cut fiber length of 89 to 140 mm
Document 2: suspend temporarily the duty on certain warp knit open-work fabric
Document 3: extends temporarily the suspension of duty on 4 Chloro Aniline
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on certain fiberglass sheets used in flooring
Document 5: authorizes the President to designate certain luggage and travel articles as eligible for duty-free treatment
Document 6: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic filament tow
Document 7: suspend temporarily the duty on certain Christmas tree lamps

topic.name: Privacy and Surveillance Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents addresses various legislative measures aimed at regulating privacy, surveillance, and data protection in the context of law enforcement and national security.

Document 1: prohibit intentionally: (1) intercepting geolocation information pertaining to another person;
sets penalties for violations.
Document 2: establish minimization and destruction procedures governing the acquisition, retention, and dissemination of any records
requires the AG to inform Congress of such requests.
Document 4: require a federal court to determine which filings, motions, and affidavits... shall be submitted ex parte
prohibits an in camera hearing, however, based on the assertion of the state secrets privilege.
Document 5: require a governmental entity operating a public unmanned aircraft system to minimize the collection or disclosure of covered information
prohibits a government entity from operating a public unmanned aircraft system and collecting or disclosing covered information for a law enforcement purpose.
Document 6: requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to promulgate regulations requiring each covered entity... to implement policies and procedures regarding information security practices
establishes procedures to be followed in the event of an information security breach.

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts and amendments aimed at reforming and improving Medicare services and benefits.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
prospective payment system (PPS) for home health services
Document 2: Bureau of Labor Statistics of the Department of Labor
computation of cost-of-living increases for Social Security and Medicare benefits
Document 3: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program)
limit to $10, adjusted for inflation, the monthly amount of prescription drug cost-sharing
Document 4: amends the Medicare Prescription Drug, Improvement, and Modernization Act
extend through 2021 the rural add-on
Document 5: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
allowing payments for over-the-counter medications
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act
treat as a physician, for Medicare purposes, a clinical psychologist
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code
allow qualified public safety employees to elect Medicare coverage

topic.name: Healthcare Policy and Access
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving healthcare access, benefits, and management in the United States.

Document 1: Adds a new title XXII to the Social Security Act entitled 'AmeriCare Health Benefits.'
Makes all U.S. residents eligible for AmeriCare benefits.
Document 2: Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act.
Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to conduct a national public education campaign on planning for care near the end of life.
Document 3: Amends part D of title XVIII (Medicare) to require each contract entered into with a prescription drug plan sponsor to prohibit certain contracts with pharmacy benefits managers.
Requires a PBM to provide a particular aggregate average reimbursement rate for generics.
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) to state that supervision requirements for therapeutic hospital outpatient services may be met under general supervision.
Directs the Secretary to establish a process for designating therapeutic hospital outpatient services.
Document 5: Removes the $44,000-per-individual limit on authorized payments by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs.
Requires the Director of the VA's Office of Rural Health to develop a five-year strategic plan.
Document 6: Amends the Public Health Service Act to deem a health professional volunteer providing primary health care at a community health center to be an employee of the Public Health Service.
Sets forth conditions for liability protection for health professionals.
Document 7: Amends the Social Security Act to establish the American Health Benefits Program to provide comprehensive health insurance coverage to all Americans.
Establishes an independent Health Benefits Administration.

topic.name: Federal Regulatory Amendments
topic.description: This cluster discusses various amendments and acts related to federal regulations and guidelines across different sectors including health, environment, and communication.

Document 1: Amends the Metric Conversion Act of 1975 to require guidelines on government use of the metric system of measurement
assigning such person the financial responsibility for failure to follow such requirements.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to assess and collect fees related to generic drugs
Requires the Secretary to report to Congress on the progress of the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in achieving specified safety, access, and transparency goals.
Document 3: This bill amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to replace the requirement that the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) prioritize review of breakthrough medical devices
The FDA must provide a summary of the basis for its determination regarding designation.
Document 4: Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) from proposing, finalizing, implementing, or enforcing any regulation revising the national primary ambient air quality standard
Defines 'nuisance dust' as particulate matter generated from natural sources, unpaved roads, agricultural activities.
Document 5: Requires federal agencies to review their covered rules and modify, consolidate, or terminate such rules on a periodic basis
Provides for sunset review of a rule upon the request of a congressional committee.
Document 6: This bill amends the Clean Air Act to lengthen from 5 years to 10 years the intervals at which the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is required to review and revise air quality criteria
The bill delays the review by an independent scientific review committee of NAAQS for ozone concentrations.
Document 7: Requires the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to develop and implement a program to encourage and permit the continued broadcasting in the analog television service
Prohibits analog broadcasting on any spectrum that is approved or pending approval for public safety radio services.

topic.name: Agricultural and Rural Development Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at enhancing agricultural practices, rural development, and related technology advancements in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make state grants for extension, education, applied research, and development activities.
Authorizes funds for rural technology transfers.
Document 4: Provides for the inclusion of locally or regionally produced agricultural food products under specified agricultural, rural development, agricultural research, crop insurance, and nutrition programs.
Directs the Federal Crop Insurance Corporation to offer a whole farm adjusted revenue risk management insurance plan.

topic.name: Employment and Immigration Policies
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to employment assistance, immigration control, and the protection of vulnerable populations within the context of U.S. law.

Document 1: employment assistance voucher program
subsidy from the state for the wages paid to the employee
Document 2: criminal background check
prohibit the employment of an individual who refuses to consent to a background check
Document 3: unaccompanied alien child
placed in removal proceedings
Document 4: Canadian citizen who is at least 50 years old
maintains a Canadian residence
Document 5: operational control of the border with Mexico
workplace enforcement, including an electronic employment verification system
Document 6: National Border Neighborhood Watch Program
increases criminal penalties for first-time illegal entry
Document 7: longer visa validity periods
premium visitor visa processing

topic.name: Federal Procurement and Accountability
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing accountability, transparency, and opportunities for small businesses in federal procurement processes.

Document 6: Authority is permanently extended for funding the DOD Acquisition Workforce Development Fund
DOD is required to provide mandatory training for members of the armed forces and DOD employees responsible for specified procurement-related market research.

topic.name: U.S. Foreign Policy and Sanctions
topic.description: This cluster discusses various U.S. legislative actions related to foreign policy, sanctions, and international relations, particularly concerning Iran, Belarus, and security measures.

Document 1: Iran Sanctions Relief Oversight Act of 2015
the monetary value of any sanctions relief that Iran has received
Document 2: Furthering International Nuclear Safety Act of 2011
encourage parties to more systematically assess their progress in improving safety
Document 3: Belarus Democracy and Human Rights Act of 2011
support radio, television, and Internet broadcasting to the people of Belarus
Document 4: prohibits a federal agency from using federal funds
until a joint resolution is enacted into law approving the most recent number of refugee admissions
Document 5: directs the President to publish in the Federal Register a list of all U.S. and foreign entities that have invested more than $20 million in Iran's energy sector
prohibits future investment in any entity included on the list
Document 6: Stop Outsourcing Security Act
ensure that only government personnel provide security services at U.S. diplomatic or consular missions in Iraq
Document 7: Foreign Surveillance Expedited Review Act
provides standing for civil actions for declaratory and injunctive relief to persons who refrain from electronic communications

topic.name: Tax Policy and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at amending tax codes and policies to provide financial relief, incentives, and adjustments for individuals and businesses.

Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase the rates and maximum allowable amount of the tax credit for employer-provided child care facilities.
Allow a new $2,000 tax credit for child care providers who hold a bachelor's degree in early childhood education.

topic.name: Legislative Acts on Crime and Justice
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at addressing crime, justice, and related social issues in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes appropriations to the Department of Justice for FY2009-FY2013 for the employment of 30 additional agents of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI)
coordinate prosecution of mortgage fraud cases with the offices of the U.S. Attorneys
Document 2: Places specified restrictions on the sale, transfer, or use of cluster munitions.
Document 3: Amends the federal judicial code to provide for an additional judgeship for the western district of Michigan.
Document 4: Allows any person being held under military authority in the part of Cuba leased to the United States to challenge the circumstances and legality of that person's detention.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of State and the Commissioner of Social Security to continue to work with the governments of the states of the former Soviet Union to encourage them to adopt policies that would allow receipt of pensions.
Document 6: Amends the Farm Security and Rural Investment Act of 2002 to increase, and extend through FY2012, funding for the biodiesel fuel education program.
Document 7: Directs the Attorney General to expand resources for the Project Gunrunner initiative of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives (ATF) to identify, investigate, and prosecute firearms trafficking.

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving public health, safety, and welfare across various demographics and issues.

Document 1: Garrett Lee Smith Memorial Act Reauthorization of 2010 - Amends the Public Health Service Act to reauthorize and revise a research, training, and technical assistance resource center to prevent suicides.
Expands the program's focus from youth suicides to suicides among all ages.
Document 2: Senior Financial Empowerment Act of 2010 - Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to disseminate to seniors and their caregivers information on mail, telemarketing, and Internet fraud targeting seniors.
Document 4: Better Health in the Arctic Act - Directs the Arctic Research Commission to examine the science base, gaps in knowledge, and strategies for the prevention and treatment of mental, behavioral, and physical health problems.
Document 5: Marine Mammal Research and Response Act of 2016 - This bill reauthorizes through FY2022 and revises the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972, including by expanding the Act and the John H. Prescott Marine Mammal Rescue and Response Grant Program.

topic.name: Alternative Energy Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at promoting and supporting the use of alternative and renewable energy sources through tax credits, grants, and regulatory amendments.

Document 1: allow an excise tax credit for alternative fuels
provide funding to improve the performance, efficiency, and integration of natural gas powered motor vehicles
Document 2: expands the urbanized area formula grants program to include public transit projects
authorizes the Secretary of Transportation to award an additional grant for public transportation equipment
Document 3: direct the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission to review decisions regarding balancing authority
publish a cost-benefit analysis of proposed decisions
Document 4: reauthorize a diesel emissions reduction program
provide grants for reducing diesel emissions from vehicles
Document 5: direct the Secretary of Agriculture to make a grant for energy generation in rural communities
authorize grants to convert power production from fuel oil to liquefied natural gas
Document 6: extend tax credits for producing electricity from renewable resources
expand the energy tax credit to include investment in advanced battery equipment
Document 7: prohibit franchisors from restricting franchisees from selling renewable fuel
establish a grants program for universities to demonstrate renewable fuel feedstock

topic.name: Mortgage and Financial Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses regulations and legislation related to mortgage lending, appraisal practices, and financial stability in housing markets.

Document 1: prohibit a creditor from extending a mortgage to any consumer without first obtaining a written appraisal
declares unfair or deceptive specified property appraisal practices
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to make loans to a financing agent
establishes the City of Detroit Growth and Stability Fund
Document 3: amends the Bank Holding Company Act relating to prohibitions on proprietary trading
prohibits any construction of these prohibitions to require the divestiture of collateralized debt obligations
Document 4: appoints the Federal Housing Finance Agency as receiver of the Federal National Mortgage Association
requires repayment by the FHFA to the General Fund of the Treasury
Document 5: amends the Truth in Lending Act to create a safe harbor from requirements for an escrow or impound account
Consumer Financial Protection Bureau is required to exempt mortgage servicers
Document 6: increases the government-wide small business procurement contract goal
requires the total value of all prime contracts for a fiscal year to include the value of each contract awarded
Document 7: grants eligible mortgagors subject to foreclosure proceedings the right to continue to occupy foreclosed properties
subject to the payment of fair market rent for a period of 20 years

topic.name: Land Management and Conveyance
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts related to the management, conveyance, and protection of federal and tribal lands.

Document 1: convey, without consideration, to Leon County, Florida, all interest of the United States in a parcel of Bureau of Prisons land
exact acreage and legal description of the property to be conveyed to be determined by a survey
Document 2: take into trust for the benefit of the Samish Indian Nation specified parcels of land
Prohibits the Tribe from conducting gaming on such land
Document 3: establish a nationally significant federal lands and tribal projects program
fund construction, reconstruction, or rehabilitation of nationally significant federal lands and tribal transportation projects
Document 4: Designates specified federal lands as wilderness
requires the Special Management Areas to be managed to maintain their presently existing wilderness character
Document 5: make a lifetime National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass available to veterans
veterans who were separated from military service under conditions other than dishonorable
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of the Interior to acquire by donation or by purchase
revise the boundary of Homestead National Monument to reflect the acquisition
Document 7: authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to accept the offer
requires the Secretary to transfer to the Secretary of the Interior administrative jurisdiction over the non-federal land

topic.name: Education Policy and Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving education through funding, resources, and support for diverse student populations.

Document 1: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to replace the program providing incentive payments to public elementary schools for foreign language instruction with a partnership program in foreign languages.
Requires such partnerships to establish, improve, or expand model programs of foreign language learning from kindergarten through grade 12.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Education to make competitive demonstration grants to institutions of higher education for up to half the cost of installing approved fire suppression systems in student housing.
Gives grant priority to applicants that demonstrate the greatest financial need.
Document 3: Amends the Scholarships for Opportunity and Results Act to require the Secretary of Education to use funds appropriated under the Act that are carried over from one fiscal year to the next to provide opportunity scholarships to eligible students.
Authorizes grants for the improvement of special education services to be used for the planning, design, and initial implementation of state programs.
Document 4: Directs the Department of Education to implement a demonstration project providing competitive, 4-year grants to secondary schools that have a 4-year adjusted cohort graduation rate of 60% or lower.
The additional school counselors must serve primarily students identified as being at risk of not graduating in four years.
Document 5: Amends title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to provide up to $17,500 of loan forgiveness to highly qualified elementary and secondary school teachers who are employed in a teacher shortage area.
Document 6: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to schools that are receiving school improvement funds.
Requires grantees to find innovative ways to encourage rapid bidirectional communication between schools and students' physicians.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish an in-school facility innovation program contest to encourage institutions of higher education to solve the problem of how to improve Indian school facilities.
Amends the school improvement program to require states to develop standards-based assessments and classroom lessons that accommodate diverse learning styles.

topic.name: Social Welfare Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at providing social support and financial assistance to individuals and families in need.

Document 1: Family Leave Insurance Act of 2008 - Directs the Secretary of Labor to establish a Family and Medical Insurance Program, mandatory for certain covered employers.
Entitles eligible employees to Program benefits that include specified percentages of their daily earnings for 12 workweeks of leave under the Family and Medical Leave Act of 1993.
Document 2: establishes a Puerto Rico Reconstruction Finance Corporation to accept applications from the government of Puerto Rico or its municipalities to restructure their bond debts.
The corporation may make expenditures to address Puerto Rico's humanitarian crisis and restore economic growth.
Document 3: Removes educational loans from the list of debts that are non-dischargeable in bankruptcy.
Prohibits the collection of amounts individuals owe the Department of Education under title IV of the HEA through offsets of social security, tax refunds, or wage garnishment.
Document 4: direct the Secretary of Education to establish a program to refinance the unpaid principal, accrued unpaid interest, and late charges on student loans.
Sets the interest rate on the refinanced loans based on the applicable categorization.
Document 5: amends the Internal Revenue Code, with respect to the low-income housing credit, to rename the credit 'the affordable housing credit' and make several modifications.
increases state allocations for the credit and modifies the cost-of-living adjustments.
Document 6: Amends the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008 with respect to producers, including peanut producers, with 10 or fewer base acres.
Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to ensure that no penalty is assessed against producers for failure to submit reports or comply with other program requirements.
Document 7: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 with respect to the state-established individual emergency unemployment compensation account.
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Actions
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various federal legislative measures, including safety regulations, facility designations, and resource studies.

Document 1: Directs the Administrator of the Federal Aviation Administration to issue an order to require the installation of secondary barriers
Defines 'covered aircraft' to mean a commercial aircraft
Document 2: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 6937 Village Parkway in Dublin, California, as the 'James 'Jim' Kohnen Post Office'
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 16150 Aviation Loop Drive in Brooksville, Florida, as the 'Sergeant Lea Robert Mills Brooksville Aviation Branch Post Office'
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to conduct a special resource study to evaluate the significance of the Newtown Battlefield
as well as the suitability and feasibility of the Battlefield's inclusion in the National Park System.
Document 5: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to contract with an independent reviewing authority to review the efficiency and effectiveness of operations
Prohibits the Secretary from merging any county Rural Development office with a county office of the Natural Resources Conservation Service
Document 6: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 522 North Central Avenue in Phoenix, Arizona, as the 'Ed Pastor Post Office'
Document 7: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 4320 Blue Parkway in Kansas City, Missouri, as the 'Wallace S. Hartsfield Post Office Building.'

topic.name: Veterans Benefits and Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at enhancing benefits and support for veterans, service members, and their families.

Document 1: Amends title II of the Social Security Act to apply a specified earnings test for blind beneficiaries.
Directs the Commissioner of Social Security to develop procedures to avoid underpayment of benefits.
Document 2: Amends federal veterans' benefits provisions for former prisoners of war.
Adds diabetes (type 2) to the diseases presumed to be service-connected.
Document 3: Sets the business standard mileage rate for travel allowances for Armed Forces members.
Authorizes transport of additional motor vehicles for members with dependents.
Document 4: Requires institutions of higher education to refund tuition for servicemembers called to military service.
Provides servicemembers an opportunity to reenroll with the same academic status.
Document 5: Makes permanent the authority of the VA to provide care to veterans involved in chemical and biological warfare testing.
Extends educational assistance under the Montgomery GI Bill.
Document 6: Revises the definition of 'qualified veteran' for the work opportunity tax credit.
Requires coordination of veteran job training services among various departments.
Document 7: Repeals provisions requiring offset of dependency and indemnity compensation from SBP annuities.
Restores annuity eligibility to surviving spouses who transferred eligibility to children.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts concerning abortion procedures, regulations, and the rights of unborn children and survivors of abortion.

Document 1: Unborn Child Pain Awareness Act of 2011
requires an abortion provider to inform the woman of the probable age of the child
Document 2: Born-Alive Abortion Survivors Protection Act
requires health care practitioners to exercise the same degree of care as reasonably provided to any other child born alive
Document 3: Pregnant Women Health and Safety Act
requires a physician who performs an abortion to have admitting privileges at a local hospital

topic.name: Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: The documents collectively address various aspects of energy policy, regulation, and environmental considerations in the context of energy development and resource management in the United States.

Document 1: Streamlining Permitting of American Energy Act of 2012 - Title I: Application For Permits to Drill Process Reform
Amends the Mineral Leasing Act to revise requirements for the issuance of permits to drill in energy projects on federal lands.
Document 2: Emergency Drought Relief Act of 2014 - Requires the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the Secretary of Agriculture, the Secretary of Commerce, and the Secretary of the Interior to provide the maximum quantity of water supplies possible
to address the emergency conditions.
Document 3: Species Rescue Act - Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to provide for expedited consultation and conferencing with respect to impacts on endangered or threatened species
directs the Secretary of the Interior or of Commerce, as appropriate, acting through the Fish and Wildlife Service.
Document 4: American Energy Renaissance Act of 2014 - Amends the Natural Gas Act regarding natural gas exports
Amends the Energy Policy and Conservation Act (EPCA) to repeal the authority of the President to restrict exports of coal, petroleum products, natural gas.
Document 5: Deficit Reduction, Job Creation, and Energy Security Act - Requires the Secretary of the Interior to conduct oil and gas lease sales under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act
Establishes the Deficit Reduction Energy Security Fund.
Document 6: Asset Forfeiture Responsibility Act of 2011 - Establishes the Fisheries Investment Fund as a separate account in the general fund of the Treasury
directs that fees deposited in the Fund be used to conduct Fund audits and specified enforcement and monitoring activities.
Document 7: American-Made Energy and Infrastructure Jobs Act - Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act (OCSLA) to direct the Secretary of the Interior to implement a leasing program
Requires the Secretary to develop and submit a new five-year oil and gas leasing program.

topic.name: Tax Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code aimed at providing tax relief and incentives for specific groups and situations.

Document 1: repeal the 85 percent maximum tax rate on Social Security benefits
appropriates amounts equal to the reduction in revenues
Document 2: work opportunity tax credit for hiring an individual receiving assistance as a victim of domestic abuse
Document 3: tax deduction for contributions of artistic properties to tax-exempt organizations
limits the amount of the deduction based upon the donor's artistic adjusted gross income
Document 4: extend through 2008 the taxpayer election to deduct state and local sales taxes
in lieu of state and local income taxes
Document 5: no gain shall be recognized on the receipt of a capital gain dividend
if such dividend is automatically reinvested
Document 6: tax credits for veterans who purchase a franchise
requires the SBA and VA to provide information about the tax credits
Document 7: increase in the Hope Scholarship tax credit
disregard of tax refunds for means tested federal programs
